You are the final reviewer in a multi-model autolabeling pipeline for nuScenes multi-view driving videos.

## Task
This multi-view driving video combines CAM_FRONT_LEFT, CAM_FRONT, CAM_FRONT_RIGHT. The following agent classes may affect the ego vehicle behavior: animal, barrier, bicycle, bus, car, construction_vehicle, motorcycle, pedestrian, traffic_cone, trailer, truck. Identify every agent in this video that is causal for the ego vehicle behavior, and report when each one affects the ego vehicle. When multiple agents of the same class overlap in time, report them as one entry covering the combined interval. Reply with one entry per causal agent or same-class group using the format: {class}: from <start_seconds> to <end_seconds> seconds.

## Video metadata
- Camera views: CAM_FRONT_LEFT, CAM_FRONT, CAM_FRONT_RIGHT (stitched multi-view video)
- Video duration: 19.75 seconds
- Candidate agent classes: animal, barrier, bicycle, bus, car, construction_vehicle, motorcycle, pedestrian, traffic_cone, trailer, truck

## Model proposals to review
Three upstream models already watched this same video and produced candidate causal-agent labels.
Treat them as hypotheses to verify, not as ground truth.

1. **Grounded-VideoLLM** (fine-tuned Phi-3.5 scene-agent model):
   Causal agents: car: from <0.00> to <5.43> seconds; pedestrian: from <7.90> to <10.37> seconds.

2. **Gemma4-12B-IT** (zero-shot, chain-of-thought prompt):
   Causal agents: car: from <0.00> to <13.00> seconds; pedestrian: from <13.00> to <18.00> seconds; truck: from <13.00> to <18.00> seconds.

3. **InternVL3.5-14B** (zero-shot, chain-of-thought prompt):
   Causal agents: pedestrian: from <0.00> to <5.75> seconds; car: from <0.00> to <10.00> seconds.

## Your review procedure
Review the video and the three proposals internally. Do not print your review notes.

**Rules**
- Trust direct visual evidence over model vote count.
- Keep only agents that are both causal for ego behavior and visually grounded.
- Merge overlapping intervals for the same causal agent class when they describe one continuous influence period.
- Reject agents that are merely present but do not influence ego decisions.
- Use precise start/end seconds within [0.00, 19.75].

## Output format (strict)
Your entire response must be exactly one line and nothing else:
Causal agents: {class}: from <start_seconds> to <end_seconds> seconds; ...
- Use seconds with two decimal places.
- Separate multiple agents with semicolons.
- If no agent is causal, reply exactly: Causal agents: none.
- Forbidden: markdown, bullet points, analysis, explanations, or any text before/after the final line.

synthesis_prompt:
You are the final reviewer in a multi-model autolabeling pipeline for nuScenes multi-view driving videos.

## Task
This multi-view driving video combines CAM_FRONT_LEFT, CAM_FRONT, CAM_FRONT_RIGHT. The following agent classes may affect the ego vehicle behavior: animal, barrier, bicycle, bus, car, construction_vehicle, motorcycle, pedestrian, traffic_cone, trailer, truck. Identify every agent in this video that is causal for the ego vehicle behavior, and report when each one affects the ego vehicle. When multiple agents of the same class overlap in time, report them as one entry covering the combined interval. Reply with one entry per causal agent or same-class group using the format: {class}: from <start_seconds> to <end_seconds> seconds.

## Video metadata
- Camera views: CAM_FRONT_LEFT, CAM_FRONT, CAM_FRONT_RIGHT (stitched multi-view video)
- Video duration: 20.05 seconds
- Candidate agent classes: animal, barrier, bicycle, bus, car, construction_vehicle, motorcycle, pedestrian, traffic_cone, trailer, truck

## Model proposals to review
Three upstream models already watched this same video and produced candidate causal-agent labels.
Treat them as hypotheses to verify, not as ground truth.

1. **Grounded-VideoLLM** (fine-tuned Phi-3.5 scene-agent model):
   Causal agents: pedestrian: from <15.54> to <20.05> seconds.

2. **Gemma4-12B-IT** (zero-shot, chain-of-thought prompt):
   Causal agents: car: from <0.00> to <19.00> seconds.

3. **InternVL3.5-14B** (zero-shot, chain-of-thought prompt):
   Causal agents: car: from <10.00> to <15.00> seconds.

## Your review procedure
Review the video and the three proposals internally. Do not print your review notes.

**Rules**
- Trust direct visual evidence over model vote count.
- Keep only agents that are both causal for ego behavior and visually grounded.
- Merge overlapping intervals for the same causal agent class when they describe one continuous influence period.
- Reject agents that are merely present but do not influence ego decisions.
- Use precise start/end seconds within [0.00, 20.05].

## Output format (strict)
Your entire response must be exactly one line and nothing else:
Causal agents: {class}: from <start_seconds> to <end_seconds> seconds; ...
- Use seconds with two decimal places.
- Separate multiple agents with semicolons.
- If no agent is causal, reply exactly: Causal agents: none.
- Forbidden: markdown, bullet points, analysis, explanations, or any text before/after the final line.
prediction_raw: Causal agents: car: from <13.00> to <19.00> seconds.
prediction_seconds: Causal agents: car: from <13.00> to <19.00> seconds.
